audit_items.proof_trace: Sync request-response path bypasses empty kzg_commitments check. While gossip validation (line 359 of peerdas_helpers.nim) rejects empty kzg_commitments, the sync path only calls verify_data_column_sidecar_inclusion_proof (line 190), which validates Merkle proof but not length, allowing storage of invalid data columns.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Attacker responds to DataColumnsByRoot requests with data columns having empty kzg_commitments. Empty list has valid Merkle root, passes inclusion proof check, gets stored in quarantine and DB, then served to other peers.
